name: Little Haiti Housing Association, INC.
description_short: Haitian American Community Development Corporation provides affordable housing, homeownership counseling, and financial education to underserved communities in South Florida since 1987.
description_long:
Haitian American Community Development Corporation (HACDC) was founded in 1987 by community members addressing the critical shortage of affordable housing in Miami's Little Haiti neighborhood. Over more than three decades, the organization has expanded its mission and geographic reach to serve diverse communities throughout South Florida while maintaining its core commitment to economic empowerment and housing stability.

HACDC offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost programs designed to build financial capability and increase homeownership. Their Homeownership Training Program (HOP) provides workshops and personalized counseling to guide prospective homebuyers through the acquisition process. They also manage affordable rental properties, conduct financial literacy workshops covering budgeting, credit management, and financial decision-making, and organize community-building initiatives. The organization appears to provide both affordable financing and subsidized financing options, though specific APRs and loan terms are not detailed on their website.

What distinguishes HACDC is its deep community roots and culturally-informed approach to serving the Haitian American population. The organization combines housing solutions with financial education and community engagement rather than operating as a single-service provider. Their three-decade track record and expansion beyond their original neighborhood suggests sustainable, trusted operations within South Florida's underserved communities.

As a non-profit housing and financial education organization, HACDC is best suited for individuals seeking affordable housing pathways, first-time homebuyers needing counseling, and community members wanting to improve financial literacy. The main limitation is that their website provides limited specificity about loan amounts, interest rates, eligibility requirements, and geographic service areas beyond South Florida, which may require direct contact for detailed program information.
pros: Established non-profit with 35+ years of community history and demonstrated trust in Little Haiti and South Florida
Free homeownership counseling through HOP program designed to guide first-time buyers
Comprehensive financial education workshops covering budgeting, credit, and informed decision-making
Offers both affordable rental options and affordable financing to address multiple housing needs
Culturally-focused mission serving the Haitian American community with deep community roots
Community-building initiatives that strengthen neighborhoods beyond just transactional services
Expanded service area beyond original Little Haiti focus shows organizational growth and sustainability
cons: Website lacks specific loan amounts, APR ranges, or financing terms for affordable financing products
Eligibility requirements, income limits, and application process details not clearly outlined
Geographic service area vaguely described as 'South Florida' without specific coverage boundaries
Limited information about counselor credentials or whether they are HUD-approved or NFCC-certified
No contact information, office hours, or clear application process provided on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in South Florida seeking free or low-cost homeownership counseling
Haitian American community members and underserved populations seeking affordable rental housing
Individuals wanting financial literacy education on credit management and budgeting
diagnosis: HACDC is best for first-time homebuyers and underserved South Florida residents seeking free or low-cost homeownership counseling and financial education from a trusted non-profit with deep community roots. The main caveat is that the website provides insufficient detail on specific loan products, eligibility criteria, and service boundaries—direct contact with the organization is necessary to determine program suitability.
services: Homeownership Training Program (HOP) workshops and personalized counseling
Affordable rental property management and leasing
Financial education workshops on budgeting and credit management
Community-building initiatives and neighborhood engagement events
Housing counseling and homeownership readiness assessment
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
